mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending earnings revisions, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and consumer credit indicates broad-based demand expansion that lifts corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days only when both series rise concurrently.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline by at least 0.05 Rank IC, or concurrent rises in HOUST and RSAFS are followed by negative SPX returns in > 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects capacity utilization recovery that boosts profit margins and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days specifically when output expands from depressed levels.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns show no significant difference between high and low INDPRO growth periods, or the signal reverses direction during supply-side bottlenecks.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signals strengthening aggregate demand that improves corporate earnings trajectories, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in higher near-term cash flows.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or quarterly GDP revisions do not correlate with subsequent 5-day SPX direction.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5 percent, tighter financial conditions compress equity risk premiums and dampen valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral when FEDFUNDS exceeds the 4.5 percent threshold, or the threshold crossing fails to predict downward price momentum in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong employment growth and stable unemployment indicates a resilient labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days only when PAYEMS rises while UNRATE remains flat or declines.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models, or PAYEMS growth combined with stable UNRATE is followed by negative SPX returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances signal aggressive household borrowing that fuels near-term consumption and equity demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days specifically when credit expansion occurs alongside low inflation.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not significantly higher when RSAFS expands in low-CPI periods, or credit growth consistently precedes equity drawdowns due to debt sustainability concerns.
